mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar XKR engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned XKR engine typically costs £4,000–£7,000 supply only, with the 5.0-litre AJ133 variant at the higher end. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 depending on the specialist. Customers have reported dealer quotes of around £15,000–£30,000 for engine replacement, while reconditioning or rebuilding is often in the £8,000–£10,000 range. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your XKR.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar XKR engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which engine era your XKR has. The 4.0-litre AJ26S (1998–2002) is the riskiest — plastic timing chain tensioners can fail catastrophically. The 4.2-litre AJ34S (2002–2006) is widely considered the most reliable — 'very robust' with coolant valley pipes as the only real weakness. The 5.0-litre AJ133 (2007–2014) offers the most power but suffers from timing chain tensioner issues — pre-2013 cars are most at risk.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar XKR?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 4.0-litre AJ26S: plastic timing chain tensioners that can fail catastrophically. The 4.2-litre AJ34S: rubber coolant valley pipes that degrade with age. The 5.0-litre AJ133: timing chain tensioner failure — pre-2013 cars most at risk. All engines: supercharger coupler wear (rattling at idle — aftermarket coupler costs around £20).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an XKR engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the vehicle value and engine era. Against a well-kept X150 worth £20,000–£30,000, a £6,000–£8,000 recon is 20–30% of the car's value — often worth it. Against a tired X100 with significant body issues, the same recon may exceed our 60% Jaguar threshold — get a scrap valuation first. The 4.2-litre cars are generally considered the most reliable and command a premium.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar XKR engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: X100 (1998–2002): AJ26S (4.0 SC). X100 (2002–2006): AJ34S (4.2 SC). X150 (2007–2014): AJ133 (5.0 SC). Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain tensioners, or does the engine need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The tensioners and guides must be replaced if worn. For the 4.0-litre, upgrading to metal tensioners is essential (£800–£2,000). For the 5.0-litre, a timing chain kit with revised components is required (£2,000–£4,000). A snapped chain almost always destroys the engine. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This XKR? Check These First
buyIf: * 4.2-litre X100 (2002–2006) — considered the most reliable XKR engine
* 4.0-litre X100 with upgraded metal timing chain tensioners
* 5.0-litre X150 (2013–2014) with revised timing chain components
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain longevity)
* No cold-start rattling (timing chain and tensioner health)
avoidIf: * 4.0-litre X100 with no timing chain tensioner history — assume it's overdue
* 5.0-litre X150 pre-2013 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — budget for timing chain work
* No service history — all XKR engines are sensitive to oil quality
* Overheating history or unexplained coolant loss — cooling system damage may have occurred
* Any XKR with a history of timing chain failure — walk away

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The XKR is Jaguar's supercharged V8 grand tourer — a 370–510 bhp masterpiece that delivers effortless performance and distinctive style. The engine choice defines the ownership experience. The 4.0-litre AJ26S (1998–2002) is the riskiest — plastic timing chain tensioners can fail catastrophically. The 4.2-litre AJ34S (2002–2006) is widely considered the most reliable — "very robust" with coolant valley pipes as the only real weakness. The 5.0-litre AJ133 (2007–2014) offers the most power (510 bhp) but suffers from timing chain tensioner issues — pre-2013 cars are most at risk, with a class action settlement reached for 2010-2016 vehicles. With typical XKR values ranging from £10,000 (early X100) to £30,000+ (late X150), an engine replacement is a major financial commitment — the 4.2-litre X100 (2002–2006) with documented maintenance is the safest choice for reliability-focused buyers.

cta.label: → Compare XKR engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar XKR (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking (timing chain tensioner warning — all engines) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (AJ26S 4.0, AJ34S 4.2 or AJ133 5.0) and oil-change discipline
* Timing Chain Tensioners — For 4.0: confirm upgrade to metal tensioners; for 5.0 pre-2013: check for chain work or cold-start rattle
* Coolant Level — Check reservoir when cold; any low or loss may indicate valley pipe leaks (4.2) or water pump issues
* Coolant Valley Pipes — For 4.2: check for any history of replacement (age-related failure)
* Supercharger Health — Listen for rattling at idle (coupler wear) — aftermarket coupler costs around £20
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve covers and oil filter housing for leaks
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes read; unresolved issues may indicate timing, cooling or sensor problems
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
